channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Career Justice Department officials reassigned to different positions: reports
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department reportedly moved several senior officials to other divisions ahead of the Senate's anticipated confirmation of President Trump's attorney general pick.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican senators issue warning to historic museums ahead of pro-life march: ‘Respect’ free speech
item.description: Sens. Cruz and Young urge D.C. museums to respect First Amendment rights of pro-life protesters after Smithsonian kicked out students in 2023 for wearing pro-life gear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Trump revokes John Bolton's Secret Service detail amid Iranian death threats: former national security advisor
item.description: The former national security advisor has faced threats from Iran going back years, including an alleged assassination plot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Ohio and Florida Senate-appointees sworn in as Vance and Rubio’s replacements
item.description: Two new senators were sworn in on Tuesday to replace new Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s federal health website scrubs ‘abortion’ search results
item.description: The most “relevant” results that come up in a search of “abortion” on HHS.gov, the website for the federal Department of Health and Human Services, are several years old, from the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Marco Rubio became secretary of state without a hitch. Keeping Trump's favor may be harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For Marco Rubio, the easy part is over. He was confirmed by the Senate as secretary of state with unanimous support, Democrats joining Republicans in praising his acumen and judgment.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump urged to pardon 76-year-old grandmother, others in prison for pro-life activism
item.description: Anti-abortion groups are calling on the president to pardon a 76-year-old grandmother and 20 others who were imprisoned and prosecuted for pro-life protests under the Biden Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-backed Montana case based on controversial election law theory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday turned back an election law case out of Montana that relied on a controversial legal theory with the potential to change the way elections are run across the country.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons upend massive Jan. 6 prosecution by freeing rioters and dismissing cases
item.description: Rioters locked up for their roles in the Jan. 6 Capitol attack have been released, while judges have begun dismissing dozens of pending cases after President Donald Trump’s sweeping grant of clemency to all 1,500-plus people charged in the insurrection.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court declines to step into former GOP Rep. Steve King's meme fight
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to revive former Rep. Steve King’s (R-Iowa) bid to receive attorneys’ fees in a copyright fight that stemmed from a meme posted by his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Expert: Trump can’t override Minnesota state law with executive orders
item.description: University of Minnesota political science professor Kathryn Pearson says President Donald Trump can’t direct states in matters that are up to state law.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Texas could join Louisiana with a law to require the Ten Commandments in classrooms
item.description: Similar proposals are in multiple states after a court ruling in 2022, though opponents say the move would impose one religious view on people with different religious traditions
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider / Houston Public Media, MPR News

item.title: Trump to make historic move towards revoking birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump wants to reinterpret the phrasing of the 14th Amendment to mean that the federal government would not recognize automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents without legal status.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: News site focused on Elon Musk to launch
item.description: Musk News, a new twice-weekly news site, will focus on the billionaire and Trump advisor's influence and actions over the course of the new presidency.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
